text: Large language models, or LLMs, are advanced AI systems that understand and generate human language. They work by analyzing enormous amounts of text from the internet, books, and articles, learning patterns and relationships between words. This training data allows them to predict the next word in a sentence, creating coherent responses. To use an LLM effectively, people employ prompt engineering—carefully crafting questions or instructions to get the desired output. These models can write essays, translate languages, and answer questions. However, they have limits: they may produce incorrect facts, lack true creativity, and can't distinguish reality from fiction. They reflect biases in their training data, so critical thinking is essential when using them.
